chapter: Chapter: ANOTHER PEEK BEYOND THE POINT (3D VISUALIZATION)

questions.question: Can a polyhedron have 10 faces, 20 edges, and 15 vertices? Justify using Euler's Formula.
questions.answer: No. $F+V-E = 10+15-20 = 5$. Since $5 \neq 2$, such a polyhedron cannot exist.

questions.question: If you slice a cube through three of its adjacent vertices, what is the shape of the cross-section?
A prism has 30 edges. How many vertices and faces does it have?
questions.answer: In an $n$-gonal prism, $E=3n$. So $3n=30 \Rightarrow n=10$. Vertices ($2n$) = 20, Faces ($n+2$) = 12.

questions.question: Name the solid formed by rotating a semicircle $360^{\circ}$ about its diameter.
What is the minimum number of faces a pyramid must have?
A net consists of a central square with four equilateral triangles attached to its sides. Which solid does this represent?
Determine the number of faces in a pyramid that has 18 edges.
questions.answer: In an $n$-gonal pyramid, $E=2n$. So $2n=18 \Rightarrow n=9$. Faces ($n+1$) = 10.

questions.question: If three cubes of side 2 cm each are joined end-to-end, what is the top view of the resulting solid?
questions.answer: A rectangle of dimensions 6 cm × 2 cm.

questions.question: Can a cross-section of a cylinder ever be a square? Explain.
questions.answer: Yes, if a vertical cut is made and the height of the cylinder equals its diameter.

questions.question: How many vertices are there in a frustum of a pentagonal pyramid?
questions.answer: 10 vertices (5 on the top base and 5 on the bottom base).

questions.question: State the number of vertices for a solid with 14 faces and 36 edges.
What shape is the vertical cross-section of a cone passing through its apex?
A solid is viewed from the top as a circle and from the front as a rectangle. Name the solid.
If a polyhedron is 'Regular', what two conditions must it satisfy?
questions.answer: 1. All faces are congruent regular polygons. 2. Same number of faces meet at each vertex.

questions.question: Find the number of edges in an $n$-gonal pyramid.
How many dimensions does a 'Shadow' typically have?
Identify the solid: It has 1 curved surface and 2 circular flat faces.
If we cut a sphere through any plane, what is always the shape of the cross-section?
A prism has 15 faces. How many vertices does it have?
What is the front view of a hexagonal pyramid held vertically?
How many edges meet at a single vertex of an Octahedron?
Can a net of a cylinder be made using only two circles? Explain.
questions.answer: No, it also requires a rectangle for the curved lateral surface.

questions.question: A polyhedron has $V=6$ and $E=12$. Is it a cube? Justify.
questions.answer: No. For a cube, $V=8$. Using $F+V-E=2$, $F+6-12=2 \Rightarrow F=8$. This is an Octahedron.

questions.question: If you slice a square pyramid horizontally (parallel to the base), what shape is the cross-section?
How many lines of symmetry does the front view of a sphere have?
questions.answer: Infinite (since the front view is a circle).
